overview.description: Arulmigu Uthanda Velayudhaswamy temple (Arulmigu Uttaṇṭa vēlāyudhasāmy tirukkōvil) is a historic 9th century old Hindu temple located on the Uthiyur Hills near Uthiyur in the Tiruppur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is dedicated to lord Velayudhasamy, a form of Kartikeya and his consorts Valli and Deivanai. The temple is located at the hilltop of the ancient Hills patronised by Arunagirinathar authored Thiruppugal. It is one of the most prominent temples of Lord Muruga in Tirupur district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Uthanda VelayudhaSamy Temple, Uthiyur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Uthanda VelayudhaSamy Temple, Uthiyur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Uthanda VelayudhaSamy Temple, Uthiyur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Coimbatore International Airport (55 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (62 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Uthanda VelayudhaSamy Temple, Uthiyur at a relaxed pace.
